name: Lianna Stroster, EMILY's List
subject: Our training team is coming to Philadelphia! Have you thought about running for office?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by EMILY's List [www
body:
|
[ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c93ae/24c69fd9/2c2c0d0f8/18abe39c/2603980691/VEsH/ "http://emilyslist.org/run-to-win?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WRENE605_digital_email_20190604_RtWPhiladelphiaTraining_v1s2&source=WRENE605_digital_email_20190604_RtWPhiladelphiaTraining_v1s2")

---

| | You're invited to our candidate training in Philadelphia >> Peter,

Run to Win is coming to Philadelphia!

In July, the EMILY's List Training team will be in Pennsylvania to train women on the basics of running for office and we want you to join us.

Run to Win 101 is a free, half-day introductory training for women thinking about running for office.

You don't need to be a declared candidate or even actively planning to run to attend. You just have to have an interest in learning the basics of being a candidate for state or local office and a passion to improve your community.

**[Apply to attend our Run to Win 101 training in Philadelphia on Saturday, July 13th, 2019.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c93ae/24c69fd9/2c2c0d0f8/18abe39d/2603980691/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/s/20190713_email_RunToWinPhiladelphia?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Apply+to+attend+our+Run+to+Win+101+train&utm_campaign=WRENE605_digital_email_20190604_RtWPhiladelphiaTraining_v1s2&source=WRENE605_digital_email_20190604_RtWPhiladelphiaTraining_v1s2")** **WHAT:**
Run to Win 101
A free, half-day training for pro-choice Democratic women thinking about running for elected office

**WHEN:**
Saturday, July 13th, 2019
9:00 a.m. -- 2:30 p.m.

**WHERE:**
Philadelphia, PA [](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c93ae/24c69fd9/2c2c0d0f8/18abe39e/2603980691/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/s/20190713_email_RunToWinPhiladelphia?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3&utm_campaign=WRENE605_digital_email_20190604_RtWPhiladelphiaTraining_v1s2&source=WRENE605_digital_email_20190604_RtWPhiladelphiaTraining_v1s2") We'll guide you through the nuts and bolts of planning your run, teach you how to get your campaign in order, and get you ready to take the first steps toward running -- and winning.

Space is limited and seats are being offered on a first-come, first-served basis.

**Submit your application to attend Run to Win 101:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Apply-For-Run-To-Win-Philadelphia](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c93ae/24c69fd9/2c2c0d0f8/18abe39f/2603980691/VEsC/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/s/20190713_email_RunToWinPhiladelphia?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=4+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgApplyForRunToWin&utm_campaign=WRENE605_digital_email_20190604_RtWPhiladelphiaTraining_v1s2&source=WRENE605_digital_email_20190604_RtWPhiladelphiaTraining_v1s2")**

Hope to see you there,

Lianna Stroster
Senior Director of National Training, EMILY's List

P.S. Can't make the training but know someone who might be interested? Forward this email to them!

|
---|---|---

name: Dick Durbin
subject: Breaking: the House just passed the Dream Act
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Dick Durbin
body:
#
Peter,
As the original sponsor of the Dream Act in 2001, I've been fighting to protect Dreamers for nearly twenty years. And I won't stop fighting until we finally pass this legislation.
Today, the House voted to pass the Dream Act, bringing us one step closer to permanently protecting our Dreamers from being deported from the only country they know.
Now, this bill heads to the Senate and we need to keep up the momentum.
**[Stand with Dreamers. Add your name now to tell the Senate to pass the Dream Act.](http://action.dickdurbin.com/page/m/45ff4ca2/42ab3cf4/636842ad/135d6ddb/2471114878/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjM0MjIzIiwiSlNWR1NWSlRWRTVCVFVVbEpRPT0iOiJQZXRlciJ9/ "https://durbin.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/tell-the-senate-to-pass-the-dream-act?source=em190604&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&firstname=Peter")**
Dreamers are men and women who were brought to this country as children, attending our schools, and pledging allegiance to our flag. They are as American as you and me. They belong here.
Unfortunately, Republicans have blocked our efforts to protect Dreamers for years. It is time to put politics aside and do what's right. That's why I'm urging my colleagues in the Senate to pass the Dream Act right away.
**[It's long past time for Congress to finally protect our Dreamers. Sign your name and urge the Senate to do the right thing.](http://action.dickdurbin.com/page/m/45ff4ca2/42ab3cf4/636842ad/135d6ddb/2471114878/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjM0MjIzIiwiSlNWR1NWSlRWRTVCVFVVbEpRPT0iOiJQZXRlciJ9/ "https://durbin.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/tell-the-senate-to-pass-the-dream-act?source=em190604&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&firstname=Peter")**
My best,
Dick Durbin
**[Add My Name](http://action.dickdurbin.com/page/m/45ff4ca2/42ab3cf4/636842ad/135d6ddb/2471114878/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjM0MjIzIiwiSlNWR1NWSlRWRTVCVFVVbEpRPT0iOiJQZXRlciJ9/ "https://durbin.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/tell-the-senate-to-pass-the-dream-act?source=em190604&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&firstname=Peter")**

* * *
Paid for by Friends of Dick Durbin

name: Cory Booker
subject: We need 400 donors from 20 states. Will you be one of them?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cory 2020
body:
The DNC raised the donor threshold and to ensure a spot on the debate stage, we need at least 400 new donors from 20 states. It's going to take a lot of resources to reach more people that believe in | | |
---
| Friend, I'm about to ask you to donate to our campaign, but first I want to explain why. The DNC raised the donor threshold and, in addition to 130,000 unique donors, we need at least 400 donors from 20 states to qualify for the debate stage this fall. The debates are where the make or break moments happen, and for a grassroots campaign like ours, we can't afford to miss them. Our campaign is proudly rejecting donations from corporate PACs and federal lobbyists, which means this is a campaign built and fueled by the people. Reaching new donors takes a lot of resources and can be tricky for an underdog campaign like ours. We don't have millionaire backers on speed dial that can write a check to find these new donors. Instead, we rely on campaign donors. Can you step up so we can make the debate stages? [_**Donate $1 now to make sure we have all the resources available to reach new donors and make it to the debate stage >>**_](https://act.corybooker. "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-20190604-em2?refcode=em_fr_20190604_CB_20190604-fr-CB-400dnr-actives_actives_l1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=") I believe that we can build a country where no one is forgotten, and no one is left behind. An America where there are good paying jobs with good benefits in every neighborhood. And an America in which we will achieve sweeping reform of our broken criminal justice system. These are just some of the issues that'll be at the forefront of the debates. And if we can make the stage, we'd get a chance to compete on a level playing field with campaigns that have raised tens of millions of dollars. [_**So I 'm asking, can you donate **__**$1 **__**and help us reach new donors to secure a spot on the debate stages?**_](https://act.corybooker. "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-20190604-em2?refcode=em_fr_20190604_CB_20190604-fr-CB-400dnr-actives_actives_l2&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=") Fighting for you, Cory
[](secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-20190604-em2?refcode=em_fr_20190604_CB_20190604-fr-CB-400dnr-actives_2020dnr_contribbutton&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=)
---

---

This campaign is moving quickly. If you want the most up-to-date information, follow Cory on [Instagram](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc092f/5fca0d19/21655fc/291b43ed/3348019806/VEsC/ "https://www.instagram.com/corybooker/"), [Twitter](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc092f/5fca0d19/21655fc/291b43ec/3348019806/VEsD/ "https://twitter.com/corybooker"), and [Facebook](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc092f/5fca0d19/21655fc/291b43eb/3348019806/VEsA/ "https://www.facebook.com/corybooker").
It's time for leadership that can reignite our spirit of common purpose. That takes all of us. We believe these emails are an important way to stay in touch, but if at any point you'd like to stop getting these updates, you can unsubscribe [here](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc092f/5fca0d19/21655fc/291b43ea/3348019806/VEsB/ "https://act.corybooker.com/unsubscribe").

name: Cory Booker
subject: There isn't a single county in America where a full-time worker making minimum wage can afford a 2-bedroom apartment at fair-market rent.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cory 2020
body:
Today, I'm releasing my plan to combat the housing affordability crisis many Americans face. | | |
---
| Friend, When I was young, my parents were looking to move into a neighborhood in New Jersey with great public schools, but real estate agents refused to sell us a home because of the color of our skin. And that would have been that -- except a group of activists came together to help my family. A young Black activist who was the head of the Fair Housing Council and a group of white volunteers and lawyers who had watched the courage of Civil Rights marchers and were inspired to help Black families in their own community came together. They stood up against the illegal housing discrimination that my parents faced -- and they won. Over 20 years ago, I moved to the Central Ward of Newark to fight slumlords and help families stay in their homes. I began my career taking the housing crisis head-on. **And today, I 'm releasing my plan to combat the housing affordability crisis many Americans face.** I want to make sure working Americans have access to safe, affordable housing. Here's how we do it:
1. Increasing the supply of affordable housing by funding the construction of new units for low-income renters and investing in rural America and Indian Country.
2. Combating discrimination and predatory practices in the housing market, including a new "Eviction Right to Counsel Fund" to help lower-income tenants fight unscrupulous landlords.
3. Eliminating homelessness by funding anti-homelessness grants and making the U.S. Interagency Council on Homelessness permanent.

[_**If you agree that everyone deserves a safe and affordable place to live, join me by adding your name.**_](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0929/5fca0d19/21f3f33/291b4270/3721390756/VEsH/ "https://act.corybooker.com/page/s/safe-and-affordable-housing?source=em_20190605") We're living in a time where there isn't a single county in America where a full-time worker making minimum wage can afford a 2-bedroom apartment at fair-market rent. That's unacceptable. My housing plan includes a Renters Credit which would help more than 57 million Americans cover their rent costs and would give a benefitting family a median credit of $4,800 to help pay for rent. 1 in 6 renting households spend more than half of their monthly income on rent -- that's too many people struggling to get by. I refuse to turn a blind eye to it. [_**The fight for fair and affordable housing is one we can 't back away from. Will you add your name and join me by signing on to my housing plan?**_](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0929/5fca0d19/21f3f33/291b427f/3721390756/VEsE/ "http://act.corybooker.com/page/s/safe-and-affordable-housing?source=em_20190605") We can do this. Together, Cory
[](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0929/5fca0d19/21f3f33/291b427f/3721390756/VEsF/ "http://act.corybooker.com/page/s/safe-and-affordable-housing?source=em_20190605")

| | **One year ago, I saw the horrors of Donald Trump's child imprisonment and family separation policies with my own eyes.** I tried to get access to the facility where _children _were being jailed. The cops were called on me. That didn't deter our movement. We shined a light on the cruelties happening in our names, at our borders. One reason this has been allowed to happen? A compliant U.S. Senate that refuses to challenge Trump on his atrocities. [**Please donate $3 today. We must win in 2020, so the people responsible for these actions _never_ have power again.**](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2a5cc/6cd27893/4c0e5950/15805788/524730329/VEsE/p/eyJlM3RoYzJ0OWZRPT0iOiIzIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjl2Ym1WOWZRPT0iOiI1IiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjkwZDI5OWZRPT0iOiIxNSIsImUzdGhZbUZ0ZEY5MGFISmxaWDE5IjoiMjUiLCJlM3RoWW1GdGRGOW1iM1Z5ZlgwPSI6IjUwIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjltYVhabGZYMD0iOiIxMDAifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?refcode=2019-06-04-homestead1year&recurring=1&amount=3&amounts=5,15,25,50,100") Here's what I wrote one year ago:
> _Donald Trump has implemented a horrific child-snatching policy. He has instructed immigration agents to rip children from the arms of their parents. These are families who are fleeing to America because their lives are at risk at home._
I wish I could tell you today that this battle was over, that we've won, that every family is reunited and that every child is free and out of jail. **But, we've made a big dent in their plans: the nation is now in an uproar over what has happened. But that hasn't fully stopped Team Trump, who still maintains child prisons in places like Homestead.** [**This is a human rights disaster caused by Republican politicians and Donald Trump. To bring it to an end, we _must_ defeat them at the ballot box in 2020. Donate $3 today and let's make it happen.**](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2a5cc/6cd27893/4c0e5950/15805788/524730329/VEsF/p/eyJlM3RoYzJ0OWZRPT0iOiIzIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjl2Ym1WOWZRPT0iOiI1IiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjkwZDI5OWZRPT0iOiIxNSIsImUzdGhZbUZ0ZEY5MGFISmxaWDE5IjoiMjUiLCJlM3RoWW1GdGRGOW1iM1Z5ZlgwPSI6IjUwIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjltYVhabGZYMD0iOiIxMDAifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?refcode=2019-06-04-homestead1year&recurring=1&amount=3&amounts=5,15,25,50,100") I'm more committed to this fight than ever. **I'm not going to stop until every last child prison is closed, until families are reunited, and until people are held accountable for the children who have died.** I've said it before. _And I'll keep saying it until this fight is won_ : for almost a century and a half, the Statue of Liberty has been a beacon to those fleeing oppression. **Trump and the Republicans have taken America to a dark place. It's up to us to relight Lady Liberty 's torch and make America the place we know it can and should be.** [**I know that you are horrified, as I am, at the evil being perpetrated against children. We need to win the White House and the Senate so America stands for hope, opportunity, and justice -- not hate and division. Donate today. Let's make it happen.**](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2a5cc/6cd27893/4c0e5950/15805788/524730329/VEsC/p/eyJlM3RoYzJ0OWZRPT0iOiIzIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjl2Ym1WOWZRPT0iOiI1IiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjkwZDI5OWZRPT0iOiIxNSIsImUzdGhZbUZ0ZEY5MGFISmxaWDE5IjoiMjUiLCJlM3RoWW1GdGRGOW1iM1Z5ZlgwPSI6IjUwIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjltYVhabGZYMD0iOiIxMDAifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?refcode=2019-06-04-homestead1year&recurring=1&amount=3&amounts=5,15,25,50,100") _**Jeff**_ Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly: | [$5](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2a5cc/6cd27893/4c0e5950/15805789/524730329/VEsD/p/eyJlM3RpZFhSMGIyNWZiMjVsZlgwPSI6IjUifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=2019-06-04-homestead1year&refcode2=express-buttons") | [$15](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2a5cc/6cd27893/4c0e5950/15805786/524730329/VEsA/p/eyJlM3RpZFhSMGIyNWZkSGR2ZlgwPSI6IjE1In0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?express_lane=true&amount=15&refcode=2019-06-04-homestead1year&refcode2=express-buttons") | [$25](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2a5cc/6cd27893/4c0e5950/15805787/524730329/VEsB/p/eyJlM3RpZFhSMGIyNWZkR2h5WldWOWZRPT0iOiIyNSJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=2019-06-04-homestead1year&refcode2=express-buttons") | [$50](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2a5cc/6cd27893/4c0e5950/15805784/524730329/VEsO/p/eyJlM3RpZFhSMGIyNWZabTkxY24xOSI6IjUwIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=2019-06-04-homestead1year&refcode2=express-buttons")

I tried to get access to the facility where children were being jailed. The cops were called on me. That didn't deter our movement. We shined a light on the cruelties happening in our names, at our borders. One reason this has been allowed to happen? A compliant U.S. Senate that refuses to challenge Trump on his atrocities. "" Here's what I wrote one year ago: Donald Trump has implemented a horrific child-snatching policy. He has instructed immigration agents to rip children from the arms of their parents. These are families who are fleeing to America because their lives are at risk at home. I wish I could tell you today that this battle was over, that we've won, that every family is reunited and that every child is free and out of jail. But, we've made a big dent in their plans: the nation is now in an uproar over what has happened. But that hasn't fully stopped Team Trump, who still maintains child prisons in places like Homestead. "" I'm more committed to this fight than ever. I'm not going to stop until every last child prison is closed, until families are reunited, and until people are held accountable for the children who have died. I've said it before. And I'll keep saying it until this fight is won : for almost a century and a half, the Statue of Liberty has been a beacon to those fleeing oppression. Trump and the Republicans have taken America to a dark place. It's up to us to relight Lady Liberty 's torch and make America the place we know it can and should be. name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: The plan I released this morning
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BETO FOR AMERICA
body:
[1]Beto for America

This morning, we released a new plan to expand voting rights and reform
our democracy. I wanted to share it with you but I also need to ask for
your help.

Among other things, our plan would end partisan gerrymandering so we can
ensure every vote counts equally; ban PAC contributions to politicians so
we can get big money out of our politics; and enact automatic and same-day
voter registration so we can add 50 million new voters to the rolls.

Our success as a country is based on the success of this democracy, the
greatest mechanism ever devised to bring forth the full power of people.
We cannot confront the great challenges ahead of us with only half
measures or half our people. But together, we can realize the full
potential of our democracy.

Without a dime from PACs, corporations, or special interests, it’s up to
each and every single one of us to ensure we have the resources to run the
largest grassroots campaign possible – not only to defeat Donald Trump in
November of 2020 but to bring this country together in January of 2021 so
we can get after the work ahead of us.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/17630?refcode=ema20190605-34713&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1007&akid=34713%2E444211%2ED4onjE ]Make a donation now to help us bring this ambitious voting rights plan
to the White House. One contribution at a time, we’re showing what it
looks like to run a campaign consistent with the highest ideals of our
democracy.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/17630?refcode=ema20190605-34713&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1008&akid=34713%2E444211%2ED4onjE ]Contribute

Our institutions have been captured, corrupted, and corroded by those in
power. When members of Congress can choose their voters, when the Supreme
Court decides that corporations are people and money is speech, when PACs
and special interests can buy the outcomes of elections and legislation,
and when voting rights are withdrawn – we inch ever closer to becoming a
democracy in name only.

A vigorous democracy is the only check against the inertia of power. It is
the best way to call forth the power, the potential, and the genius of a
people around the challenges and the opportunities we face.

During last year’s election in Texas, we came within points of winning
because we did something absolutely transformational for the democracy. In
a state that had ranked 50th in voter turnout, we increased participation
to presidential-year levels. We saw young voter turnout up 500 percent.

This voting rights plan is what our campaign is and always has been about
– making every voice count. Listening to people, no one taken for granted
or left behind. Refusing all money from PACs, corporations, and special
interests. Rallying the American people behind the bold, ambitious ideas
that will bring more people into our democracy than ever before.

There’s more to come, but I want to leave you with this:

The only way to make progress is if every single American is empowered to
vote – and those who have historically been drawn out of our democracy are
able to make their voices heard so that this country can live up to its
full promise and potential.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/17630?refcode=ema20190605-34713&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1009&akid=34713%2E444211%2ED4onjE ]If this is a vision that you believe in, please make a donation to help
us to not only win but to reform our democracy.

There’s never been a more important, more urgent moment for us to deliver
this victory.

– Beto

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/17630?refcode=ema20190605-34713&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1010&akid=34713%2E444211%2ED4onjE ]Contribute

P.S. You can read the full plan on our website [ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/17631?source=ema20190605-34713&t=1011&akid=34713%2E444211%2ED4onjE ]here.

Contribute to Beto's campaign: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beto-for-america-emails?refcode=em20190605footer&t=1012&refcode2=34713_444211_D4onjE&akid=34713%2E444211%2ED4onjE

body:
| | Friend --
[](http://my.yang2020.com/?e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=060519_democracy_petition_2&n=1) I've been running for President for over a year now, and I've become even more convinced that moneyed interests have captured our democratic system. Especially since the decision in _Citizens United_ , the amount of money that it takes to run a campaign is ridiculous, and it ensures that the rich will have both an easier time running for office and a direct line to politicians who rely on their donations to win elections. Almost all politicians complain about needing to spend hours every day on "call time" - sitting there, reaching out to wealthy donors and pleading for their support. Why is this something we accept? Aren't our leaders supposed to spend their time fixing our problems, not catering to the whims of the wealthiest? This is a primary reason our politics has become so broken, and we have to fix it. I also believe that the way we vote has resulted in a system that no longer works for most Americans. Two of the past five elections have been won by the candidate with fewer votes. Third-party candidates frequently bring important considerations and voices to the political process, but they're drowned out by the two major parties, and - fair or not - are usually called spoilers by the losing party. I'm for overturning _Citizens United_ and reforming (though not eliminating) the electoral college, but those would require constitutional amendments. That process would take too long considering our democracy is breaking down now. We need to focus on solutions to these problems today, and we can do that through reforms to the way campaigns are funded and the way Americans vote. [If you agree that we need to get money out of politics, and change the way we vote, sign the petition here!](https://my.yang2020.com/restore_democracy?e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=060519_democracy_petition_2&n=2 "Restore Democracy") Reform #1 - Democracy Dollars The easiest way to minimize the influence of super-rich individuals and SuperPACs in our politics is to drown that money out with contributions by normal Americans. Under my Democracy Dollars plan, every American would receive $100 per election cycle to donate to candidates of their choosing. This would amount to a potential $20 billion+ a year, much higher than the amount spent by these moneyed interests if even a small proportion of Americans utilize them. It would also save our leaders from spending hours every day calling and begging for money. That's time that would be better spent looking out for the interests of the people they represent, and it would keep them human, as making those calls is soul-crushing. Reform #2 - Ranked Choice Voting A democracy requires the people to feel that their elected officials represent them. Our current, one-round voting system frequently results in that not happening. People feel they can't vote for a third-party candidate they prefer, and they often vote strategically instead of for their favorite candidate. In primaries, this often leads to more extreme candidates because centrists lose out to the wings of the party that don't represent the average voter. If we move to a ranked choice voting system, we'll end up with candidates that better represent the preferences of voters. It would reward candidates with broad support, and some studies show it decreases negative campaigning. It would also break up the two-party duopoly that currently dominates our elections. There are many other reforms I'm proposing to address many other problems with our democracy - you can read about them below. But, to me, diminishing the influence of those with the largest amount of money, and reforming our first-past-the-post voting system, will do the most good in the shortest time. reforms!](https://my.yang2020.com/restore_democracy?e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=060519_democracy_petition_2&n=3 "Restore Democracy Petition") # **Congress Needs to Back America’s Workers and Approve Trump’s Trade Deal**

“In 1993, very few Americans had a cellphone, and no one had ever heard of buying a book on Amazon”—and yet that year marked “the last time Congress meaningfully debated trade between our two North American partners,” Sen. Mike Braun (R-IN) writes in _The Daily Caller_.

That is why President Donald J. Trump recently renegotiated NAFTA, a trade agreement that’s woefully out of date. “House Democrats have spent decades fundraising against NAFTA,” Sen. Braun writes. Now “they have an opportunity to replace it with a better agreement — one that includes a minimum wage for Mexican auto workers and provisions to protect American jobs from unfairly traded Chinese products.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d64c7a1b9c&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “President Trump on Wednesday joined other world leaders and the royal family in a ceremony in Portsmouth, England, to commemorate the upcoming 75th anniversary of the D-Day invasion,” Brett Samuels reports in [_The Hill_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d1886887b0&e=3ee4985eea). “Hundreds of people -- including military officials and World War II veterans -- gathered at an amphitheater just across the English Channel from where the Allied invasion took place on June 6, 1944. Trump took the stage to read an excerpt from a prayer that then-President Franklin Roosevelt delivered to the nation on the radio on the eve of the invasion.”
---
|
---
| “President Trump and first lady Melania Trump hosted a dinner for Prince Charles and Duchess of Cornwall Camilla Parker Bowles on Tuesday night at Winfield House, a mansion in London which is the official residence of the United States ambassador to the United Kingdom,” Talia Kaplan reports for [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=da416b42b0&e=3ee4985eea). “Earlier Tuesday, Trump and the first lady toured the Churchill War Rooms with the prime minister and her husband. British leaders used the bunkers to plot strategy during World War II.”
---
|
---
| “The new tariffs on Mexico announced by President Trump are a win for the United States,” former Gov. Jan Brewer (R-AZ) writes in _[The Hill](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=fc1a8866c9&e=3ee4985eea)_. “Mexico has the ability to significantly alleviate the humanitarian and national security crisis on the southern border by strictly enforcing its own strong immigration laws to prevent ‘caravans’ of illegal immigrants from traversing its territory on their way to the United States. With Democrats obstructing his every move to limit the flow of crime and drugs over our border, President Trump is taking every measure to address the crisis on his own, hopefully resulting in Mexico becoming a part of the solution.”
---
|

name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: Time is of the essence, Peter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger For Congress
body:
On World Environment Day, we must urge Washington politicians to accept their international responsibility and lead the way forward in combating climate change
| | Peter,

Today is World Environment Day, a day designated by the United Nations to raise awareness about the threats to our environmental security and advocate for strategies to reverse those dangers.

World Environment Day is celebrated internationally in more than 100 countries, but in our own nation, **elected officials and entrenched politicians are refusing to lead on climate issues, instead abdicating our country 's seat at the global decision-making table.**

That's despite the wide-reaching consensus in our national security, military, and intelligence communities that climate change poses an ever-growing threat to international stability by exposing already vulnerable governments to conditions that will disproportionately harm civilian populations. **I have to be blunt here: We cannot wait any longer to take action.** That's why I introduced -- _and passed_ \-- legislation to maintain the US's position as a global partner in the fight against climate change and to recommit to the goals outlined in the Paris Climate Agreement.

[**Time is of the essence, Peter. Please sign my petition today, on World Environment Day, to urge Washington politicians to accept their international responsibility and lead the way forward in combating climate change. >>**](https://act.myngp.com/el/ZTTKtVBnM5H_OeNuwFXMdbLXD5lho6R7xpwEb2Vim-k=/-6CSclNwQlWu-Pzi5aBGYpIl6kRvWQ0-49q8kl2JH2E=) | [SIGN IMMEDIATELY »](https://act.myngp.com/el/ZTTKtVBnM5H_OeNuwFXMdbLXD5lho6R7xpwEb2Vim-k=/-6CSclNwQlWu-Pzi5aBGYpIl6kRvWQ0-49q8kl2JH2E=)
---
We've known for years now that environmental degradation puts US interests around the world in peril. In 2003, the Pentagon authorized its own report detailing how our changing environment would directly impact our global security.

Now, 16 years later, the threats of climate change are multiplying and encroaching on our own backyards: They are making our air more dangerous to breathe, disasters are tearing our communities apart, and our agricultural businesses are literally flooding.

**Yet, politicians in Washington are ignoring the risks and refusing to take action.**

As the world's foremost economic, diplomatic, and military leader, we cannot relinquish our seat at the table and our voice on this life-threatening issue. We must be present in shaping the conversation that began with the Paris Agreement.

[**Peter, join me in demanding climate action now. Sign our petition to tell Washington the time is long past due to reverse the dangerous effects of climate change and protect our national security. >>**](https://act.myngp.com/el/ZTTKtVBnM5H_OeNuwFXMdbLXD5lho6R7xpwEb2Vim-k=/-6CSclNwQlWu-Pzi5aBGYpIl6kRvWQ0-49q8kl2JH2E=)

---
Thank you for joining me in this fight.

_\-- Abigail _

She believes in leadership that flows from our shared American values of integrity, responsibility, and decency, and she wants to work to move this country forward together. subject: When it comes to women's reproductive rights it should be no one's choice but their own.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bennet for America
body:

| Women's reproductive rights are under assault all over the United States.

I will do everything I possibly can do to ensure Roe v. Wade remains the law of the land because health care decisions should be in the hands of women and their families. _Period._

But cruel legislation being passed into law in states like Alabama, Mississippi, Ohio, and Missouri rips that right away - effectively banning abortions. This is a direct threat to women's health and safety and an attack on Roe v. Wade.

name: Jill Biden
subject: I'm so grateful
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Biden for President
body:
Like teachers around the country, I just posted my grades and am getting ready for the summer. | Peter,

Like teachers around the country, I just posted my grades and am getting ready for the summer. And while I do have a summer reading list, like many teachers, I'll spend my "vacation" working. In fact, I'm headed to South Carolina today for a listening tour with educators from around the state.

As we start the next month on the campaign, I'm proud that our first major policy proposal was for the issue most near and dear to my heart: **our plan to better support America's schools, teachers, and students.**

**And I 'm so grateful that more than 8,000 educators have donated to the campaign -- the largest number of any occupation.**

Joe and I believe that education is critical to the success of our nation. He understands that the best policies don't come from politics; they come from classrooms. Joe's plan is the result of listening and learning from the educators who know what our schools need better than anyone.

That's why I'm so proud to be #TeamJoe, and why I know that so many teachers are standing beside us.

Let's start summer by beginning June strong and making it clear that educators deserve not just one partner in the White House, but two!

subject: Socialism is NOT the answer
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hickenlooper 2020
body:
Beating Donald Trump next year will require us to stand up and tell the hard truths, even when they're not popular. If you agree, will you make your first donation to help John build off this momentum | | | [](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771603/691c9bd/6c24621e/11a4e005/4008039016/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_template")

| [](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771603/691c9bd/6c24621e/11a4e002/4008039016/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_fr_20190605_BK_20190605-fr-BK-socialism-ndnr_ndnr_i1&recurring=auto&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=") Friend,

Last weekend, John got on stage at the California Democratic Party Convention and told them a hard truth:

**To defeat Donald Trump and accomplish big progressive goals, socialism is not the answer.**

A lot of people in the crowd didn't want to hear that simple truth, and responded with boos--but John didn't back down. He stood tall and declared "if we're not careful, we're going to help re-elect the worst president in history."

**But sometimes, when you lose the room, you gain a national audience.** News outlets from the _Washington Post_ to _CNN_ to _BuzzFeed_ noted how John shook up the conversation with his remarks, proving that common sense and pragmatism still matter in this race.

**Beating Donald Trump next year will require us to stand up and tell the hard truths, even when they 're not popular. If you agree, will you make your first donation to help John build off this momentum and emerge as a frontrunner in this race?** "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_fr_20190605_BK_20190605-fr-BK-socialism-ndnr_ndnr_b50&recurring=auto&amount=50&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=")** **[](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771603/691c9bd/6c24621e/11a4e03f/4008039016/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_fr_20190605_BK_20190605-fr-BK-socialism-ndnr_ndnr_b100&recurring=auto&amount=100&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=")** **[](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771603/691c9bd/6c24621e/11a4e03c/4008039016/VEsO/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_fr_20190605_BK_20190605-fr-BK-socialism-ndnr_ndnr_bother&recurring=auto&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=")** Look, we can all agree on this: We _need_ a health care system that works for all Americans. We _need_ a bold response to climate change. We _need_ an economy that puts workers and small businesses first. We need to make all of these a reality.

**But embracing socialist policies isn 't the way to get there.**

In Colorado, we got to 95% healthcare coverage, and jumped from 40th in job creation to the #1 economy in the nation. We got there by working with nonprofits and businesses, with Democrats and Republicans, and with progressive policies that worked for everyone.

**That 's what it means to be a _pragmatic_ progressive. And that's the kind of record and resolve it'll take to make Donald Trump a one-term president.**

[**_If you 're ready to help John beat Trump with a message that resonates with the majority of the American people, chip in now._**](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771603/691c9bd/6c24621e/11a4e03d/4008039016/VEsP/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_fr_20190605_BK_20190605-fr-BK-socialism-ndnr_ndnr_l1&recurring=auto&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

Thank you,
Brad

name: Team Beto
subject: Where does all that money come from?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BETO FOR AMERICA
body:
[1]Beto for America

Just hours ago, Beto rolled out a historic plan to repair our broken
democracy. One of the key pieces? Banning political action committees from
bankrolling candidates for elected office.

In 2018, more than one-third of all donations to House candidates came
from PACs. And these PACs, many of them run by corporations, have captured
so much of our government, raising over $3 billion to peddle influence and
elect sympathetic politicians.

It’s not enough to just decry the influence of big money (as many
Democrats do). We need to walk the talk. Beto has rejected PAC money for
five years and counting because he knows how special interests have
corrupted and corroded American democracy.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/17641?refcode=ema20190605-34739&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1019&akid=34739%2E444211%2EU5AfLz ]If you agree we need to walk the talk, add a $3
contribution today to help Beto run a strong campaign without taking a
dime from PACs.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/17641?refcode=ema20190605-34739&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1020&akid=34739%2E444211%2EU5AfLz ]Contribute $3

Some of these PACs represent big corporate interests. The pharmaceutical
industry, the telecom industry, the energy industry, the insurance
industry... you name it! And they’re not using that money just to get
access, although that’s part of it. They’re buying outcomes in the form of
legislation.

So when you wonder why our democracy is so dysfunctional, why it doesn’t
represent the interests of the people it purports to serve, all you have
to do is follow the money.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/17641?refcode=ema20190605-34739&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1021&akid=34739%2E444211%2EU5AfLz ]As for our campaign, we can say confidently and proudly it’s built by
ALL people, no PACs. Join us today and make a donation.

- Team Beto

And these PACs, many of them run by corporations, have captured so much of our government, raising over $3 billion to peddle influence and elect sympathetic politicians. It’s not enough to just decry the influence of big money (as many Democrats do). We need to walk the talk. Beto has rejected PAC money for five years and counting because he knows how special interests have corrupted and corroded American democracy. [ ]If you agree we need to walk the talk, add a $3 contribution today to help Beto run a strong campaign without taking a dime from PACs. [ ]Contribute $3 Some of these PACs represent big corporate interests. The pharmaceutical industry, the telecom industry, the energy industry, the insurance industry... you name it! And they’re not using that money just to get access, although that’s part of it. They’re buying outcomes in the form of legislation. So when you wonder why our democracy is so dysfunctional, why it doesn’t represent the interests of the people it purports to serve, all you have to do is follow the money. [ ]As for our campaign, we can say confidently and proudly it’s built by ALL people, no PACs. Join us today and make a donation. - Team Beto [ ]Contribute Contribute to Beto's campaign:

body:
Elizabeth's not giving anyone special access based on how much they can contribute to her campaign. Her time is not for sale, and every supporter has an equal place in our movement. | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852e604/45c30650/16a91db26/669823a1/143189633/VEsH/ "https://elizabethwarren.com/")
---
Elizabeth's already visited 19 states plus Puerto Rico this year, including Pennsylvania.

Today, she's made it to #20: Indiana. And tonight's her 95th grassroots event of the year: A live town hall with Chris Hayes on MSNBC.

There's one big reason why Elizabeth is able to visit this many states and host so many grassroots events: She doesn't stuff her schedule with private fundraisers with wealthy donors.

**We 're running a grassroots campaign, through and through -- but that means events like today's in Indiana are only possible because of supporters like you.

[We can't do it without your help. Will you chip in $3 to power this campaign and build our grassroots movement?](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852e604/45c30650/16a91db26/669823a0/143189633/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LWp1bjE5ZnIiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6IjIwMTkwNjA1ZW0yLWZ1bGwifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ew-jun19fr?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=20190605em2-full")**

Typically, candidates for president spend their time flying from big city to big city to scoop up as many big fat checks as possible. They're trying to squeeze every last dollar out of bigwig donors.

But Elizabeth decided to do things differently: She's not giving anyone special access based on how much they can contribute to her campaign. Her time is not for sale, and every supporter has an equal place in our movement.

That means she gets to spend her time the way she wants: Talking with people across the country about real solutions to real problems -- which is exactly what she'll be doing in Indiana tonight. And then getting organized at the grassroots level to make change happen.

We know swearing off private swanky fundraisers leaves money on the table. But we're running the campaign we want to run -- one that is powered by the grassroots movement behind it.

**[So let's make sure we have the biggest grassroots movement possible: Chip in for the first time and help power our campaign in the weeks and months ahead.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852e604/45c30650/16a91db26/669823a0/143189633/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LWp1bjE5ZnIiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6IjIwMTkwNjA1ZW0yLWZ1bGwifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ew-jun19fr?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=20190605em2-full")**

Thanks,

Team Warren

name: Cory Booker
subject: I want to talk to you about the DNC debates
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cory 2020
body:
| | |
---
| Team, I'll put it bluntly: Qualifying for the debates under the new DNC rules isn't going to be easy. Not only do we need to reach 130,000 individuals who have chipped in to support this campaign, but we have to demonstrate that our support is distributed across the country -- with at least 400 donors in 20 states. **I talk about this a lot because there 's no Plan B if we fall short of these thresholds. **Being on stage, talking to the entire country about issues and ideas that might otherwise get left behind -- that's the way a campaign like ours breaks through. And the truth is, unlike some other campaigns, we refuse to take checks from corporate PACs and D.C. lobbyists. We can't just cash big checks from billionaires to help find new donors. We're counting entirely on people like you. [_**It 's easy for you to help right now: Get us one step closer to both debate thresholds right now by making your first donation to the campaign -- even if it's just $1.**_](https://act.corybooker. "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-20190605-em?refcode=em_fr_20190605_CB_20190605-fr-CB-400in20-actives_2020ndnr_l1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=") We probably won't ever have the resources of some of the frontrunners. I knew from the start that I'd be an underdog in this race. But if we can compete on the same debate stage, and present our ideas to the voters on equal footing, I believe with all my heart that we can win. [_**Help me get there -- make a donation right now.**_](https://act.corybooker. "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-20190605-em?refcode=em_fr_20190605_CB_20190605-fr-CB-400in20-actives_actives_l2&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=") So grateful to be on this team with you, Cory
